questions.question: Total cash sales of $400 were received. Which transactions will then be entered into the nominal ledger? 2 and 3 onlyD2, 3 and 4C1 onlyB1 and 4A An account in the purchases ledger of X Limited has a debit balance. What does this balance represent? 4 an amount owed by X LimitedA an amount paid to X LimitedB a prepayment made by X LimitedC total purchases made by X LimitedD 11_7707_12_2025 2 The following account appeared in the books of Mary.5 Paul account $$ 25returnsApril 2190balance b/dApril 1 88bank30150sales14 2discount 125balance c/d___ 240240 Which statement is correct? On 1 April, Paul owed Mary $90.A On 14 April, Paul sold goods for $150 to Mary.B On 21 April, Mary returned goods for $25 to Paul.C On 30 April, Mary owed Paul $125.D A supplier has received a debit note from a customer. What does the supplier use this debit note for? 6 to complete a statement of accountA to correct the sales journalB to make an entry in the sales returns journalC to prepare a credit noteD Sue allows 2% cash discount to credit customers who settle their account within 28 days. Jane purchased goods for $200 from Sue on 10 September but returned these goods to Sue on

questions.question: September. Which entry did Sue make for the transaction on 15 September? 7 purchases returns journal $196A purchases returns journal $200B sales returns journal $196C sales returns journal $200D 11_7707_12_2025 3 A trader prepared their trial balance which showed the debit column total of $45 000 and the credit column total of $40 000. The following errors were then discovered. 8 The d iscount received account balance of $500 was listed in the debit column of the trial balance. 1 The r eturns outwards account balance of $250 was omitted from the trial balance. 2 The d ebit column of the trial balance was overcast by $3750. 3 $ 44500D All errors were corrected and the trial balance totals agreed. What were the trial balance totals after the correction of errors?, A $40 500 B $40 750 C $41 000 Ahmed’s trial balance totals didnotagree. He entered the difference in a suspense account. The following errors were later discovered. 9, A payment of $200 for a vehicle repair had been debited to the motor vehicles account. 1 Drawings of $150, correctly recorded in the cash book, had not been posted to the drawings account. 2 The sales journal had been undercast by $900.3 What was the original balance on Ahmed’ s suspense account? $1050D$950C$750B$550A 11_7707_12_2025 4 Susanne is a trader. She received a cheque from Zahir, a credit customer, for $300. This cheque was incorrectly debited to Zahir’V account and credited to the bank account. Which journal entry corrects this error? 10 credit $ debit $ 300 300bank Zahir A 600 600bank Zahir B 300 300Zahir bank C 600 600Zahir bank D Atrialbalancedoesnot agreeandasuspenseaccountisopened. Whichstatementisnot correct? 11 Errors of commission and errors of original entry are entered in the suspense account.A The trial balance difference is entered in the suspense account.B C The suspense account remains open until all the errors preventing the agreement of the trial balance have been corrected. When all of the errors found have been corrected there will be no balance carried down inthe suspense account. D After receiving her bank statement, Lyn updated her cash book. Which items would reduce the overdrawn balance in the cash book? 12 1 bank interest received 2 credit transfer received 3 direct debit payment 3 onlyD2 and 3C1 and 3B1 and 2A 11_7707_12_2025 5 Natalieisbothacustomerof andasuppliertoGustav. On28June,Gustav’saccountinNatalie’spurchasesledgerhadacreditbalanceof$295, andhisaccountinNatalie’ssalesledgerhadadebitbalanceof$140. Gustavwishestooffsetoneamountagainsttheother. WhichentrieswillGustavmakeinhiscontrolaccounts? 13 creditdebit sales ledger control account $140purchases ledger control account $140 A sales ledger control account $155purchases ledger control account $155B purchases ledger control account $140sales ledger control account $140C purchases ledger control account $155sales ledger control account $155D Johnny prepared his sales ledger control account. The figures in it came from several different sources. Which statements are correct? 14 1 The source of the opening balance was the sales account. 2 The discount allowed was totalled in the three-column cash book. 3 The irrecoverable debts were posted from the general journal. 3 onlyD2 and 3C1 and 3B1 and 2A At the beginning of the year on 1 January, Zac paid $420 for an equipment repair. He entered this amount in the equipment account. At the end of the year on 31 December, depreciation of 20% per annum was charged on the balance of the equipment account, using the straight-line method. What was the overall effect on the book value of the equipment on 31 December? 15 $84 understatedA $336 overstatedB $420 overstatedC $504 understatedD 11_7707_12_2025 6 Why does a business need to provide for depreciation in its accounting records?16 to ensure availability of funds to replace its old non-current assetsA to ensure that its current assets value isnotoverstatedB to record as an expense the amount of the non-current assets used up during the yearC to show its non-current assets at their market valueD An asset originally costing $20 000 had been depreciated by $15 000 when it was sold for $3000. Which entry will be shown in the disposal account to record the amount to be transferred to the income statement? 17 $2000 creditA $2000 debitB $8000 creditC $8000 debitD Why should prepaid income be shown in the financial statements of a business?18 so that credit customers pay the correct amountA so that payments to credit suppliers are correctly recordedB so that the statement of financial position shows the correct current assetsC so that the total income is matched against the total costs of the same periodD What is the effect on total assets and on equity in the statement of financial position if the value of closing inventory is overstated? 19 equitytotal assets overstatedoverstated A understatedoverstatedB overstatedunderstatedC understatedunderstatedD 11_7707_12_2025 7 Who works in a service business?20 1 a hairdresser 2 an estate agent 3 a car mechanic 4 a car salesperson 1, 2 and 3A 1 and 2 onlyB 2, 3 and 4C 3 and 4 only D At the beginning of the year on 1 January, Keith was owed $1500 commission by clients. During the year, Keith received $18000 commission. At the end of the year on 31 December, Keith was owed $800 commission by clients. How much commission did Keith record in his income statement for the year ended 31 December? 21 $20300D$18700C$17300B$15700A Jack and Gill are setting up in business as partners and are considering writing up a partnership agreement. What is not included in any partnership agreement drawn up by them? 22 the rate of interest to be charged on drawingsA the rate of interest to be paid on a loan from a partnerB the rate of interest to be paid on a loan received from the bankC the rate of interest to be paid on the capital invested by each partnerD Raj and Rohit are in partnership sharing profits and losses in the ratio of 2:1. Raj is entitled to an annual salary of $3000. The profit for the year was $14100 for the year ended 31 December 2024. On 1 January 2024, Raj’s current account had a debit balance of $1800. What was the credit balance on Raj’s current account on 1 January 2025? 23 $12400D$12200C$10400B$8600A 11_7707_12_2025 8 BCD Limited provided the following information.24 $ 300000 200000 170000 ordinary shares retainedearnings debentures How much was the equity and the capital employed of BCD Limited? capital employed $ equity $ 470000300000 A 670000300000B 470000500000C 670000500000D Which statement correctly describes called-up share capital?25 all share capital which has been issued to shareholdersA shares which have been issued and thosenotyet issuedB the share capital for which payment has been requestedC the value of shares for which payment has been receivedD, A sports club was established on 1 January. It has a clubhouse and also runs a snack bar for its members. Which items would be included in the receipts and payments account for the first year ended

A sports club was established on 1 January. It has a clubhouse and also runs a snack bar for its members. Which items would be included in the receipts and payments account for the first year ended

questions.question: December? 26 1 new furniture to be used in the clubhouse, paid by credit transfer 2 subscriptions paid by club members in advance for the next financial year 3 subscriptions owing by club members at the end of the financial year 4 snack bar wages owing at the end of the financial year 3 and 4D2 and 3C1 and 4B1 and 2A 11_7707_12_2025 9 A sports club started running a café, selling hot and cold drinks and confectionery, on 1 January 2024.27 $ 3200sales 1750purchases 500wages paid to café assistant 250inventory at 31 December 2024 What is the profit on the café for the year ended 31 December 2024? $1450D$1200C$950B$700A, A manufacturing business provided the following information.28 $ 236 000 42000 8000 6000 prime cost factory overheads opening work in progress closing work in progress What was the factory cost of production transferred to the income statement?, A $234 000 B $238 000 C $276 000 D $280 000 Gem has been in business for many years. On 1 March, Gem purchased a new factory. On that date, he paid three months’ insurance on his factory, totalling $900. What should Gem enter for insurance in his statement of financial position on 31 March? 29 $300 in current assetsA $300 in current liabilitiesB $600 in current assetsC $600 in current liabilitiesD 11_7707_12_2025 10 The following information is given for the year ended 31 December 2024. Munira has not maintained a full set of accounting records. How would she calculate her credit purchases figure for a month? 30 A B C payments to credit suppliers + closing trade payables – discounts received – opening trade payables payments to credit suppliers – closing trade payables + discounts received + opening trade payables payments to credit suppliers – closing trade payables – discounts received + opening trade payables payments to credit suppliers + closing trade payables + discounts received – opening trade payables D The assets of a business increased by $22 000 and its liabilities decreased by $6000 over a one-year period. During this period, the owner invested an additional $4000 of private funds into the business and withdrew $7000 for personal use. How much was the profit for the year? 31 $31000D$19000C$17000B$13000A Jonny provided the following information.32 $ 3500inventory 100cash 1900bank overdraft 2400trade receivables 2100trade payables 1.95:1D1.58:1C What was Jonny’s current ratio?, A 1.33 : 1 B 1.50 : 1 11_7707_12_2025 11 Jose runs a business providing accounting and book-keeping services. What isnotrelevant in analysing his business financial statements? 33 current ratioA profit marginB rate of inventory turnoverC return on capital employedD Jamila’s business sells one type of product only. She provided the following information.34 year 2year 1 10001000number of units sold $10800$8000sales revenue 31 days28 daysrate of inventory turnover What happened in year 2? The sales price decreased, and goods were sold faster.A The sales price decreased, and goods were sold more slowly.B The sales price increased, and goods were sold faster.C The sales price increased, and goods were sold more slowly.D Antionette decides to treat a calculator bought for her large business as an expense instead of as a non-current asset even though it will be used for several years.
